title: How to expose existing R code as a web service
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"579se","text":"This protocol shows how to expose any existing R method as a web service. The code shown here has been used as part of the development of the CoExp Web Application https://rytenlab.com/coexp/. For further information on CoExp Web, please see our paper: https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fgene.2021.630187/full","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":164,"length":27},{"key":1,"offset":253,"length":67}],"data":[]},{"key":"drsm0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"201io","text":"The R methods used in this protocol correspond to the family of CoExpNets R packages (https://github.com/juanbot/CoExpNets).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":86,"length":36}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"D62D64AE97A211EB88FA0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://rytenlab.com/coexp"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"10DE835A97A311EB88FA0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fgene.2021.630187/full"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"A38CA52897A211EB88FA0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://github.com/juanbot/CoExpNets"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"aosgq","text":"All steps and code snippets shown in this protocol have been tested in a machine with the following characteristics:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5v5tt","text":"Operating system: CentOS Linux 7 (Core)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"69ju1","text":"R version 3.5.1 (2018-07-02) -- \"Feather Spray\".","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5uoh3","text":"CoExpNets R packages installed.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":20}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"E2AD3E4097A311EB85A197D87F6345D2","url":"https://github.com/juanbot/CoExpNets"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"drs8k","text":"Two dockerized versions of CoExp - backend and frontend - can be found on https://hub.docker.com/r/soniaruiz/coexp","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":74,"length":40}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"21C06EAD987911EBA4C30A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://hub.docker.com/r/soniaruiz/coexp"}}}}
steps: First, we install the R package Plumber . To install the latest stable version from CRAN, we can type the following R command: install.packages("plumber")

The next step will consist of creating the API specification to expose our R methods of interest. In this case, we are going to expose the method 'CoExpNets::getNetworkCategories()' from the CoExpNets family of R packages ( https://github.com/juanbot/CoExpNets ). This step might sound daunting, but it is very straightforward: we only need to generate an R file to include on it all methods we would like to expose through the web API. In this example, the contents of the new API R file (let's call it 'api.R') should look as follows: #' @get /getNetworkCategories #this is the name we will type as part of the URL on our browser when calling the API method function(){ print("getNetworkCategories() GET method has requested!") # a friendly message CoExpNets::getNetworkCategories() # calling the R method }

Once changes have been saved, the next step would consist of running the web API. To do so, we first need to choose an empty port, open an R console and type the following commands: ## Code to load CoExpNets libraries library(CoExpNets) library(CoExpROSMAP) CoExpROSMAP::initDb(mandatory=T) ## Code to expose the API library(plumber) pr("/pathtoyourfile/api.R") %>% pr_run(port=8080)

Finally, we test the API: curl "localhost:8080/getNetworkCategories" Also available via URL: http://localhost:8080/getNetworkCategories In both cases, the response should be similar to: ["CoExpROSMAP"]
creator: Sonia García-Ruiz
